concerns.text: Tasks T045 (dynamic features) and T046 (plasticity proxy) are listed in Phase 3 (US1) but depend on T019 (graph metrics) which is also in Phase 3. While T045/T046 are marked as dependent on T019 in the 'Specific Ordering Requirements' section, they are not strictly ordered in the task list itself relative to T019. More critically, T047 and T048 (US2) depend on T045/T046, but T047/T048 are listed in Phase 4 (US2) which is intended to be independent of US1. This creates a hidden cross-story dependency that violates the 'independently-shippable unit' requirement for User Stories. T047/T048 cannot be implemented until T045/T046 (US1) are complete, breaking the parallel execution model.

Task T047 (integrate dynamic features) and T048 (feature importance analysis) are placed in Phase 4 (US2) but depend on T045/T046 (US1). This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule across user stories. US2 tasks should not depend on US1 implementation details unless explicitly designed as a sequential dependency, which contradicts the 'Independent Test' and 'Parallel Team Strategy' sections stating US2 can start after Foundational phase independently.

Task T040 (memory profiling wrapper) is listed after T019 and T020. T020 already implements explicit validation for memory usage. T040 is described as a 'lightweight memory-profiling wrapper' around T019. If T020 validates the limit, T040 is redundant or should be integrated into T019/T020. As a separate task, it creates an unnecessary ordering dependency (T040 after T019) for a feature that should be part of the core implementation (T019) or validation (T020).

The 'Specific Ordering Requirements' section lists T045/T046 must be executed after T019, and T047/T048 after T045/T046. However, the task list itself does not reflect this strict ordering visually (e.g., T045 is listed after T020 but before T040). The explicit dependency text contradicts the visual grouping in Phase 3/4, causing confusion about whether US2 can truly start in parallel with US1.
